title: Add support for mounting external containers
body:
Continue progress on use of external containers.

This PR adds the ability to mount, umount and list the
storage containers whether they are in libpod or not.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
This is now passing tests.

This very much smells like a future blog post once it's merged.
Changes LGTM, but definitely want a @mheon head nod.

@edsantiago Since I don't believe podman mount -a should mount external containers, podman umount -a should not unmount them.  I added documentation of this to the podman mount and podman umount man pages.  PTAL

>@edsantiago Since I don't believe podman mount -a should mount external containers, podman umount -a should not unmount them. I added documentation of this to the podman mount and podman umount man pages. PTAL

I 100% agree about `mount -a`, but I'm still concerned about `umount`. If I as a user run `podman umount -a`, I really expect it to unmount everything listed by `podman mount`. I can envision situations in which a user runs a script or tool which (unbeknownst to them)  does an external mount and leaves it behind. The user then tries to clean up and is stymied because they can't easily unmount. I find it jarring and confusing. If you feel strongly about `umount -a` not umounting -a, would you consider adding a warning like "will not unmount external container xyz"??
I am willing to go along with umount -a unmounting all, my only argument against is based on symmetry.  I think for the most part very few if any users will ever do mount -a and unmount -a, anyways.
@edsantiago podman umount -a will now unmount --external containers.
